# UC-13.1.8 · Deployment Server Status

## Description

Deployment server issues prevent app/config distribution to forwarders, leaving them with stale or incorrect configurations.

## Value

Deployment server issues prevent app/config distribution to forwarders, leaving them with stale or incorrect configurations.

## Implementation

Monitor deployment server logs for errors. Track successful vs failed deployments to clients. Alert on deployment failures. Verify client phone-home intervals are within expected ranges.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Monitoring Console.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `_internal` (deployment server logs).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Monitor deployment server logs for errors. Track successful vs failed deployments to clients. Alert on deployment failures. Verify client phone-home intervals are within expected ranges.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=DeploymentServer
| search log_level=ERROR
| stats count by message, host
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Deployment Server Status** — Deployment server issues prevent app/config distribution to forwarders, leaving them with stale or incorrect configurations.

Documented **Data sources**: `_internal` (deployment server logs). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Monitoring Console. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal; **sourcetype**: splunkd. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_internal, sourcetype=splunkd. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by message, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over calm and busy windows. Compare with the Splunk monitoring console and relevant health and introspection views, and spot-check key fields in sample events.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (deployment errors), Single value (clients checking in), Bar chart (failures by server class).

## SPL

```spl
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=DeploymentServer
| search log_level=ERROR
| stats count by message, host
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (deployment errors), Single value (clients checking in), Bar chart (failures by server class).
